Code fragment: public final TableField<ExperimentRecord, Long> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.BIGINT.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");

Code fragment: public final TableField<ExperimentRecord, String> USER_NOTES = createField(DSL.name("user_notes"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR.nullable(false).defaultValue(DSL.field("''::character varying", SQLDataType.VARCHAR)), this, "");
Explanation: The USER_NOTES column uses an empty string as a default value instead of allowing NULL to represent missing data. This conflates 'no notes provided' with 'intentionally empty notes'. Consider making the column nullable and using NULL to represent missing values.
